_comment: Command Center Demo Pack fixture — the ENTIRE fictional narrative for the 'Harbor & Oak Candle Co.' demo. Fictional data only; no real client PII, no real names, no secrets. Consumed by scripts/demo/seed-demo.ts. Per-department targetInputs drive the REAL computeCompanyHealth engine (we seed the four grading INPUTS and let the engine compute the letters — grades are never faked with a grade column).

company.industry: E-commerce — hand-poured artisan candles & home fragrance

sops.name: Melted / Damaged-in-Transit Replacement
sops.steps: 1. Verify order + photo. 2. Offer replacement or refund. 3. File carrier claim. 4. Send apology + code.
sops.success: Customer replied to, replacement created, claim filed.

sops.name: New Scent Launch Playbook
sops.steps: 1. Scent story brief. 2. Photography + copy. 3. Email flow. 4. Social + paid. 5. Post-launch recap.
sops.success: Launch shipped on date with all assets QC-passed.

sops.name: Reel Production Checklist
sops.steps: 1. Hook in first 2s. 2. On-brand color grade. 3. Captions. 4. CTA. 5. Schedule.

sops.name: Fragile Glass Packing Standard
sops.steps: 1. Double-wall box. 2. Corner protectors. 3. Fragile label. 4. Photo before seal.
sops.success: Order packed to standard with proof photo.

sops.name: Paid Creative Refresh
sops.steps: 1. Pull fatigued creative. 2. Draft 3 hooks. 3. Launch test. 4. Read ROAS at 72h.

sops.name: PDP Conversion Fix
sops.steps: 1. Repro issue. 2. Fix + test mobile. 3. Ship. 4. Verify analytics.
sops.success: Change shipped and verified on mobile + desktop.

sops.name: Wholesale Lead Follow-Up
sops.steps: 1. Qualify the boutique. 2. Send line sheet + samples. 3. Follow up in 3 days. 4. Log in CRM.
sops.success: Lead qualified, samples sent, next step booked.

sops.name: On-Brand Asset Delivery
sops.steps: 1. Brief + references. 2. Draft in brand palette. 3. Internal QC. 4. Export + hand off.

campaigns.name: Harvest & Ember — Autumn Launch
campaigns.description: Cross-department push for the autumn scent collection: email, social, paid, and a gift-set bundle.

campaigns.name: Holiday Gifting 2026
campaigns.description: Corporate + retail gifting program with wholesale outreach and a limited holiday bundle.

messages.text: Morning team — harvest launch is live. Marketing and Social, keep the momentum; Fulfillment, flag the carrier delay the moment it risks the ship-by date.
Launch email went out to the win-back segment — open rate is tracking above our Q3 average.
Three pour reels queued for the week, the 'oak & sea salt' one is already outperforming.
Heads up: the carrier delay hit the harvest batch. Working the backlog and re-packing two damaged orders now.
On it from the CX side — I've got the melted-in-transit replacements moving and I'm reusing the shipping-delay macro.
Good. Sage, if the delay slips past Friday, escalate and we'll comp shipping. Everyone else, stay the course.

interview.synthesis: Harbor & Oak Candle Co. is a hand-poured, small-batch candle and home-fragrance brand selling direct-to-consumer online, with a growing wholesale side into boutiques and spas. The owner wants the brand to feel warm, grounded, and quietly premium — 'a calm room in a loud world' — and cares most about repeat customers who come back every season. They are 65% of the way through onboarding: identity and most of branding are captured; the brand color and the home-base name are the last two cards before the department board.

answers.answer: E-commerce — hand-poured artisan candles and home fragrance, direct-to-consumer with a growing wholesale side into boutiques and spas.

answers.prompt: Do you have a logo? Paste a public link to it (or upload one).
answers.answer: Yes — the amber flame-and-jar mark on deep forest green. It's already loaded into the workspace.

answers.prompt: What feeling do you want your brand to evoke?
answers.answer: Calm and grounded — like walking into a warm, quiet room after a long, loud day. Understated, not flashy.

answers.prompt: What feeling do you want your customers to leave with after working with you?
answers.answer: Cared for. Like they treated themselves to something small and real that makes their home feel like theirs.

answers.prompt: What words would your best customer use to describe you?
answers.answer: Cozy, honest, beautifully made, and 'the candle that actually smells like it says.'

answers.prompt: How would you describe your brand voice?
answers.answer: Warm and unhurried, a little poetic but never precious. We talk about scent like a story, not a spec sheet.

answers.prompt: Who is your ideal customer — and why do they come to YOU specifically?
answers.answer: Women 30–55 who make their home a sanctuary and are tired of candles that look great and smell like nothing. They come to us because our scents are true and our burn is clean — and they come back every season for the new collection.

answers.prompt: Why do people come to YOU versus anyone else who does what you do?
answers.answer: Honest scent and an honest burn. No synthetic 'fragrance oil' that fades in an hour, no tunneling. Real story, real throw, and packaging that survives the mail.

conversationalDepth.prompt: How do orders usually come in today, and where do things get stuck?
conversationalDepth.answer: Most orders are online through the website; wholesale comes through a form and email. The stuck point is fulfillment when a carrier is slow or glass gets damaged — that's our weak spot right now.

conversationalDepth.prompt: What does a great season look like for you a year from now?
conversationalDepth.answer: Repeat-purchase rate up near 40%, fulfillment complaints basically gone, and a holiday collection that sells through without me touching the day-to-day.
